examples.title: Pseudocode: Ethereum Transaction
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic steps of an Ethereum transaction
examples.code:
ALGORITHM EthereumTransaction
INPUT: sender, recipient, value, gasPrice, gasLimit
OUTPUT: transactionHash

BEGIN
 calculate transaction fee = gasPrice * gasLimit
 IF sender balance >= value + transaction fee THEN
 create transaction object with sender, recipient, value, gasPrice, gasLimit
 calculate transactionHash = hash(transaction object)
 broadcast transaction to Ethereum network
 RETURN transactionHash
 ELSE
 RETURN error: insufficient funds
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic steps of an Ethereum transaction, including calculating the transaction fee, checking the sender's balance, creating the transaction object, and broadcasting it to the network.

examples.title: Python: Ethereum Account Balance
examples.description: Demonstrates how to retrieve an Ethereum account balance using the Web3 library
examples.code:
from web3 import Web3

# Set up Web3 provider
w3 = Web3(Web3.HTTPProvider('https://mainnet.infura.io/v3/YOUR_PROJECT_ID'))

# Set up account address
account_address = '0x742d35Cc6634C0532925a3b844Bc454e4438f44e'

# Get account balance
balance = w3.eth.get_balance(account_address)

print(f'Account balance: {balance} wei')
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates how to retrieve an Ethereum account balance using the Web3 library. It sets up a Web3 provider, specifies the account address, and uses the `get_balance` method to retrieve the balance.

examples.title: Java: Ethereum Account
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple Ethereum account class written in Java
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates a simple Ethereum account class with a constructor, address field, and balance field. It provides getter methods for the address and balance.
